source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 8. ADD(HAD1417MCW) H&abyz 인증관련 자료  20220203 (CE Dimdi)/4. TCF/09. Usability Engineering File/09-01. Usability Engineering Report (Doc.No.A-TCF-5-056(rev.0)).docx

document.title: Usability Engineering Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability engineering report for the ADD Abyz Digital Detector documenting usability process, hazards, UI requirements, verification, validation references, and residual risk conclusion.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports the usability engineering file by specifying analysis, specification, design, verification, and validation activities related to safety and normal use.
keyPoints: The manufacturer is H&abyz and the product name is Abyz Digital Detector with model name ADD.
The device is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
The report references EN/IEC 60601-1-6, EN/IEC 62366, and EN/ISO 14971 standards.
Usability verification results for UI-01 through UI-07 are all recorded as Pass.
The conclusion states that overall residual risk can be accepted.

entities.name: R&D team
entities.evidence: Prepared R&D team Jung, Woosuk MAY/27/2020

entities.name: QA representative
entities.evidence: Approval QA representative Hur Namkyu MAY/27/2020

concepts.name: Usability engineering process
concepts.evidence: This document specifies a process for a manufacturer to analyse, specify, design, verify and validate usability, as it relates to safety of.

concepts.name: Normal use
concepts.evidence: This usability engineering process assesses and mitigates risks caused by usability problems associated with correct use and use errors, i.e. NORMAL USE.

concepts.name: Application specification
concepts.evidence: Application specification - Description, intended use - Medical purpose - Intended PATIENT population - Intended part of the body or type of tissue applied to or interacted with - Intended user profile - Intended conditions of use - Operating principle

concepts.name: Frequently used functions
concepts.evidence: Shall determine the frequently used functions that involve user interaction with the medical device.

concepts.name: Primary operating functions
concepts.evidence: The inputs to the primary operating functions shall include the following: - frequently used functions - Functions related to safety of the medical device.

concepts.name: Usability specification
concepts.evidence: The usability specification shall provide: - testable requirements for usability verification; and - testable requirements for usability of the primary operating functions including criteria for determining the adequacy of risk control achieved by the usability engineering process.

concepts.name: Usability verification
concepts.evidence: Shall verify the implementation of the medical device user interface design against the requirements of the usability specification.

concepts.name: Usability validation
concepts.evidence: Shall validate usability of the medical device according to the usability validation plan.

concepts.name: Residual risk
concepts.evidence: Through overall residual risk assessment, we are confirmed that overall of the residual risk can be accepted.

evidence.claim: The document is a usability engineering report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Engineering Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-TCF-5-056 Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-5-056 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The report was prepared, reviewed, and approved on MAY/27/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Jung, Woosuk MAY/27/2020 Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin MAY/27/2020 Approval QA representative Hur Namkyu MAY/27/2020

evidence.claim: Revision 0 was established on MAY/27/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 0 Rev. date MAY/27/2020 Revision contents Establishment

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer: H&abyz

evidence.claim: The product name is Abyz Digital Detector and the model name is ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name: Abyz Digital Detector Model Name: ADD

evidence.claim: The report references usability, medical electrical equipment, and risk management standards including EN/IEC 60601-1-6, EN/IEC 62366, and EN/ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1-6:2010 Medical electrical equipment Part 1-6: General requirements for safety – Collateral standard: UsabilityIEC 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2013 Medical electrical equipment Part 1-6: General requirements for safety – Collateral standard: Usability EN 62366 [2008] Application of usability engineering to medical devicesIEC 62366:2007+A1:2014 Application of usability engineering to medical devices EN ISO 14971[2012] Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devicesISO 14971 [2007] Medical devices -- Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The device is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The device is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and support real-time diagnosis and treatment planning.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis and treatment planning on a real time basis for physicians and radiologists.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The intended patient population includes adults and children, except pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: a) Age: Adult and Children ... e) Patient group/state: except pregnant women

evidence.claim: The intended use environment is professional hospital use.
evidence.evidenceText: Environment: General Hospital, intended for professional use

evidence.claim: Operating conditions are 5℃ to 40℃, 30 to 75% relative humidity non-condensing, and 700 hPa to 1060 hPa.
evidence.evidenceText: Operation condition Temperature: 5℃ to 40℃ Humidity: 30 to 75% relative humidity (non-condensing) Air Pressure: 700 hPa to 1060 hPa

evidence.claim: Storage conditions are -10℃ to 50℃, 10 to 80% relative humidity non-condensing, and 500 hPa to 1060 hPa.
evidence.evidenceText: Storage condition Temperature: -10℃ to 50℃ Humidity: 10 to 80% relative humidity (non-condensing) Air Pressure: 500 hPa to 1060 hPa

evidence.claim: General daily use is less than 9 hours per day, with worst-case continuous use for battery charging over 24 hours.
evidence.evidenceText: Frequency of use General daily use : Less than 9 hours per day Reasonably foreseeable worst case use scenarios : Assume continuous use for battery charging (using a day 24 Hours)

evidence.claim: The ADD requires periodic calibration.
evidence.evidenceText: E.2.7 Is maintenance and/or calibration necessary A The ADD will need periodic calibration.

evidence.claim: The ADD is a portable device.
evidence.evidenceText: E.2.22 Is the Medical device intended to be mobile or portable? A Portable device

evidence.claim: The ADD is intended for professional use.
evidence.evidenceText: E.2.12 Does installation or use of the Medical device require special training or special skills? A Need professional use

evidence.claim: Safe use information is provided through the user manual and label.
evidence.evidenceText: E.2.13 How will information for safe use be provided? A Provided user manual and label

evidence.claim: The device has accessories such as power supply and connector.
evidence.evidenceText: E.2.16 Does the Medical device have connecting parts or accessories? A There is some accessories to connect the device like power supply, connector etc.

evidence.claim: Functions related to safety include detector and power supply power buttons and LED indications, switch box power status indicator, and battery remaining volume indication.
evidence.evidenceText: Functions related to Safety a) Detector / Power supply - Power On/off button - LED indication ... b) Battery - Battery remaining volume

evidence.claim: Usability verification checklist items include button, accessories, LED indicator, manual, safety instruction, language, and product label.
evidence.evidenceText: UI-01 Button Power Button ... UI-02 Accessories Switch Box, Power supply, Battery Charger ... UI-03 LED indicator Detector, Power supply, Switch Box, Battery Charger, Battery ... UI-04 Manual Understanding ... UI-05 Safety instruction ... UI-06 Language Korean, English ... UI-07 Product Label Understanding

evidence.claim: Usability verification results for UI-01 through UI-07 are pass with no issues.
evidence.evidenceText: UI-01 No issues Pass UI-02 No issues Pass UI-03 No issues Pass UI-04 No issues Pass UI-05 No issues Pass UI-06 No issues Pass UI-07 No issues Pass

evidence.claim: The usability validation plan is referenced as Doc. No. A-TCF-5-039.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Plan Refer to Usability Validation Plan (Doc. No. A-TCF-5-039)

evidence.claim: The usability validation report is referenced as Doc. No. A-TCF-5-040.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Refer to Usability Validation Report (Doc. No. A-TCF-5-040)

evidence.claim: The report concludes that risk control measures are sufficient and overall residual risk can be accepted.
evidence.evidenceText: So far advanced risk management process is able to promote the safety of the product through the identification of hazards and risk control measures are sufficient. ... Through overall residual risk assessment, we are confirmed that overall of the residual risk can be accepted.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is ambiguous: source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, sourcePath references CE MDD, and the document text does not explicitly identify FDA, CE, MDD, a notified body, or a jurisdiction.
Source text contains OCR or conversion artifacts, including '오류! 책갈피가 정의되어 있지 않습니다.', malformed spacing between standard citations, and apparent typos such as 'Person suing' and 'Men by which'.

Hazard checklist evidence contains internal inconsistency: one section states no parts are intended to be sterilized, while a later hygienic context line says the device is intended to be sterilized by the user.
ProductSignal metadata lists HAD/A/F 1417-1717, but the document text identifies only product name Abyz Digital Detector and model name ADD.
